definitionEn: The name of the Latin script letter A/a.
Easy to use.
On board; into or within a ship or boat; hence, into or within a railway car.
example: We all climbed aboard.

definitionEn: Moving around; astir.
example: After my bout with Guillan-Barre Syndrome, it took me 6 months to be up and about again.

definitionEn: Heaven.
(with definite article) Something absent, especially absent people collectively; those who were or are not there.
To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.
An unexpected event with negative consequences occurring without the intention of the one suffering the consequences.
example: to die by an accident

definitionEn: A registry of pecuniary transactions; a written or printed statement of business dealings or debts and credits, and also of other things subjected to a reckoning or review.
Continued dull pain, as distinguished from sudden twinges, or spasmodic pain.
The fruit of the oak, being an oval nut growing in a woody cup or cupule.
An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day's plowing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840 square yards or 4,046.86 square meters.
(crosswords, often in combination) A word that runs horizontally in the completed puzzle grid or its associated clue.
example: I solved all of the acrosses, but then got stuck on 3 down.

definitionEn: Something done, a deed.
example: an act of goodwill

definitionEn: Something done, a deed.
example: an act of goodwill

definitionEn: An act or instance of adding.
Direction.
To be amazed at; to view with surprise; to marvel at.
The encountering of risks; a bold undertaking, in which dangers are likely to be encountered, and the issue is staked upon unforeseen events; a daring feat.
(usually used predicatively, not attributively, be afraid) Impressed with fear or apprehension; in fear.
example: He is afraid of death.

definitionEn: Later; second (of two); next, following, subsequent
The part of the day from noon or lunchtime until sunset, evening, or suppertime or 6pm.
(temporal location) At a later or succeeding time.
(temporal location) At a later or succeeding time.
Another time; once more.
In a contrary direction to.
example: It is hard work to swim against the current.

definitionEn: The whole duration of a being, whether animal, plant, or other kind, being alive.
To cause to grow old; to impart the characteristics of age to.
example: Grief ages us.

definitionEn: Gone; gone by; gone away; passed; passed away.
example: in days ago/in days agone

definitionEn: To harmonize in opinion, statement, or action; to be in unison or concord; to be or become united or consistent; to concur.
example: all parties agree in the expediency of the law.

definitionEn: The speaker or writer, referred to as the grammatical subject, of a sentence.
In or to the front; in advance; onward.
Help; assistance; succor, relief.
example: He came to my aid when I was foundering.

definitionEn: The pointing of a weapon, as a gun, a dart, or an arrow, or object, in the line of direction with the object intended to be struck; the line of fire; the direction of anything, such as a spear, a blow, a discourse, a remark, towards a particular point or object, with a view to strike or affect it.
example: Take time with the aim of your gun.

definitionEn: The substance constituting earth's atmosphere, particularly:
example: I'm going outside to get some air.

definitionEn: A powered heavier-than-air aircraft with fixed wings.
An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.
example: Because of this, and contrary to popular belief, British airports primarily act as giant wealth extractors, transporting our richest citizens out of the country as often as possible to take their money overseas. ― The Independent, Heathrow expansion to mainly benefit wealthy frequent fliers, research finds, 23 june 2018.

definitionEn: Consisting of air.
example: an airy substance; the airy parts of bodies

definitionEn: A summons to arms, as on the approach of an enemy.
Having resemblance or similitude; similar; without difference.
example: The twins were alike.

definitionEn: Having life; living; not dead
example: As long as the plant is alive, he will continue to water it.

definitionEn: (with a possessive pronoun) Everything that one is capable of.
example: She gave her all, and collapsed at the finish line.

definitionEn: A narrow street or passageway, especially one through the middle of a block giving access to the rear of lots or buildings.
example: The parking lot to my friend's apartment building is in the alley.

definitionEn: Either of two species of large amphibious reptile, Alligator mississippiensis or Alligator sinensis, in the genus Alligator within order Crocodilia, which have sharp teeth and very strong jaws and are native to the Americas and China, respectively.
example: All you could see of the alligator were its two eyes above the water, and suddenly it snatched up and caught the poor bird with its strong jaws full of sharp teeth.

definitionEn: To grant, give, admit, accord, afford, or yield; to let one have.
example: to allow a servant his liberty; to allow a free passage; to allow one day for rest

definitionEn: Something or someone that doesn't quite make it.
example: In all the submissions, they found four papers that were clearly worth publishing and another dozen almosts.

definitionEn: By oneself, solitary.
example: I can't ask for help because I am alone.

definitionEn: In company; together.
example: John played the piano and everyone sang along.

definitionEn: Spoken out loud.
Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future; by this time; previously.
(focus) In addition; besides; as well; further; too.
example: They had porridge for breakfast, and also toast.

definitionEn: At all times; throughout all time; since the beginning.
example: Airplanes did not always exist as a form of transportation.

definitionEn: Before noon.
Denotes a mingling or intermixing with distinct or separable objects. (See Usage Note at amidst.)
example: How can you speak with authority about their customs when you have never lived among them?

definitionEn: The total, aggregate or sum of material (not applicable to discrete numbers or units or items in standard English).
example: The amount of atmospheric pollution threatens a health crisis.

definitionEn: One
In rhythm, the second half of a divided beat.
An incorporeal and sometimes divine messenger from a deity, or other divine entity, often depicted in art as a youthful winged figure in flowing robes.
A strong feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something, usually combined with an urge to harm.
example: You need to control your anger.

definitionEn: Displaying or feeling anger.
example: An angry mob started looting the warehouse.

definitionEn: In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).
example: A cat is an animal, not a plant. Humans are also animals, under the scientific definition, as we are not plants.

definitionEn: An additional one of the same kind.
example: There is one sterling and here is another

definitionEn: A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.
example: Her answer to his proposal was a slap in the face.

definitionEn: Any of various insects in the family Formicidae in the order Hymenoptera, typically living in large colonies composed almost entirely of flightless females.
To even the slightest extent, at all.
example: I will not remain here any longer.

definitionEn: Any one out of an indefinite number of persons; anyone; any person.
example: Anybody will do.

definitionEn: (manner) In any way or manner whatever.
Any person; anybody.
example: Almost anyone can change a light bulb.

definitionEn: Someone or something of importance.
Regardless; anyhow.
example: He didn't enjoy washing his car, but it was so dirty that he did it anyway.

definitionEn: In or at any location or an unknown location.
example: I don't know where I left my keys. They could be anywhere.

definitionEn: (Used after a noun or in the predicate) Exceptional, distinct.
example: a world apart

definitionEn: A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
A primate of the clade Hominoidea, generally larger than monkeys and distinguished from them by having no tail.
Each by itself; for or to each one
example: These melons cost a dollar apiece.

definitionEn: To come or be in sight; to be in view; to become visible.
A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.
An article of clothing worn over the front of the torso and/or legs for protection from spills; also historically worn by Freemasons and as part of women's fashion.
To come up from a lower to a higher position.
example: to arise from a kneeling posture

definitionEn: The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
example: She stood with her right arm extended and her palm forward to indicate “Stop!”

definitionEn: The amount an arm or arms can hold.
A large, highly organized military force, concerned mainly with ground (rather than air or naval) operations.
example: The army was sent in to quell the uprising.

definitionEn: To come up from a lower to a higher position.
example: to arise from a kneeling posture

definitionEn: (with the verb "to be") Present in the vicinity.
example: Is Clare around today?

definitionEn: To set up; to organize; to put into an orderly sequence or arrangement.
To reach; to get to a certain place.
example: He arrived home for two days.

definitionEn: To reach; to get to a certain place.
example: He arrived home for two days.

definitionEn: The conscious production or arrangement of sounds, colours, forms, movements, or other elements in a manner that affects the senses and emotions, usually specifically the production of the beautiful in a graphic or plastic medium.
example: There is a debate as to whether graffiti is art or vandalism.

definitionEn: A person who creates art.
To such an extent or degree; to the same extent or degree.
example: It's not as well made, but it's twice as expensive.

definitionEn: The solid remains of a fire.
example: Ash from a fireplace can restore minerals to your garden's soil.

definitionEn: An incidental remark made quietly so as to be heard by the person to whom it is said and not by any others in the vicinity.
An act or instance of asking.
In a state of sleep; also, broadly, resting.
example: I was asleep when you called.

definitionEn: To reply to or talk to someone, either online or face-to-face. (from the practice of targeting a message or reply to someone online by writing @name)
example: Cars 2 is the most influential film of all time. Don't @ me.

definitionEn: To ingest; to be ingested.
An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
To listen to (something or someone); to pay attention to; regard; heed.
Mental focus.
example: Please direct your attention to the following words.

definitionEn: Awe-inspiring, majestic, noble, venerable.
example: an august patron of the arts

definitionEn: The sister or sister-in-law of one’s parent.
The originator or creator of a work, especially of a literary composition.
example: Have you read any Corinthian authors?

definitionEn: A type of vehicle designed to move on the ground under its own stored power and intended to carry a driver, a small number of additional passengers, and a very limited amount of other load. A car or motorcar.

Traditionally the third of the four seasons, when deciduous trees lose their leaves; typically regarded as being from September 24 to December 22 in parts of the Northern Hemisphere, and the months of March, April and May in the Southern Hemisphere.
A broad street, especially one bordered by trees.
Not asleep; conscious.
To depart; to go to another place.
example: At 9 o'clock sharp he awayed to bed.

definitionEn: Very bad.
example: My socks smell awful.

definitionEn: Badly, terribly.
example: She led after the swiming and cycling, but ran awfully and came in fourth.

definitionEn: For some time; for a short time.
example: Sit with me awhile.

definitionEn: A tool for felling trees or chopping wood etc. consisting of a heavy head flattened to a blade on one side, and a handle attached to it.
A tool for felling trees or chopping wood etc. consisting of a heavy head flattened to a blade on one side, and a handle attached to it.
The characteristic cry or bleating of a sheep.
A baby or infant; a very young human or animal.
example: These events came to pass when he was but a babe.

definitionEn: The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.
example: Could you please scratch my back?

definitionEn: One's social heritage, or previous life; what one did in the past.
example: The lawyer had a background in computer science.

definitionEn: The state behind or past.
Cured meat from the sides, belly or back of a pig.
Error, mistake.
A distinctive mark, token, sign, emblem or cognizance, worn on one's clothing, as an insignia of some rank, or of the membership of an organization.
example: the badge of a society; the badge of a policeman

definitionEn: Ill, unwell.
example: He's never badly.

definitionEn: A flexible container made of cloth, paper, plastic, etc.
The act of cooking food by baking.
A person who bakes and sells bread, cakes and similar items.
A shop in which bread (and often other baked goods such as cakes) is baked and/or sold.
A solid or hollow sphere, or roughly spherical mass.
example: a ball of spittle; a fecal ball

definitionEn: An inflatable buoyant object, often (but not necessarily) round and flexible.
An elongated curved tropical fruit that grows in bunches and has a creamy flesh and a smooth skin.
A strip of material used for strengthening or coupling.
A sudden percussive noise.
example: When he struck it with a hammer, there was a loud bang.

definitionEn: A stringed musical instrument (chordophone), usually with a round body, a membrane-like soundboard and a fretted neck, played by plucking or strumming the strings.
example: I come from Alabama with my banjo on my knee...

definitionEn: An institution where one can place and borrow money and take care of financial affairs.
One who conducts the business of banking; one who, individually, or as a member of a company, keeps an establishment for the deposit or loan of money, or for traffic in money, bills of exchange, etc.
A solid, more or less rigid object of metal or wood with a uniform cross-section smaller than its length.
example: The window was protected by steel bars.

definitionEn: A person whose profession is cutting (usually male) customers' hair and beards.
(‘the bare’) The surface, the (bare) skin.
Wearing nothing on the feet.
example: After taking off their shoes, socks and sandals at the doorway, the kids were barefoot.

definitionEn: The short, loud, explosive sound uttered by a dog, a fox, and some other animals.
A building, often found on a farm, used for storage or keeping animals such as cattle.

A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.
example: a cracker barrel

definitionEn: Something from which other things extend; a foundation.

A sport common in North America, the Caribbean, and Japan, in which the object is to strike a ball so that one of a nine-person team can run counter-clockwise among four bases, resulting in the scoring of a run. The team with the most runs after termination of play, usually nine innings, wins.
A floor of a building below ground level.
A lightweight container, generally round, open at the top, and tapering toward the bottom.
example: A basket of fake fruit adorned the table.

definitionEn: Any of the flying mammals of the order Chiroptera, usually small and nocturnal, insectivorous or frugivorous.
The quantity of bread or other baked goods baked at one time.
example: We made a batch of cookies to take to the party.

definitionEn: A tub or pool which is used for bathing: bathtub.
The act of swimming or bathing, especially in the sea, a lake, or a river; a swimming bath.
example: I'm going to have a midnight bathe tonight.

definitionEn: To clean oneself by immersion in water or using water; to take a bath, have a bath.
A room containing a shower and/or bathtub, and (typically but not necessarily) a toilet.
A contest, a struggle.
example: the battle of life

definitionEn: A berry.
The shore of a body of water, especially when sandy or pebbly.
Prayer, later especially with a rosary.
Any large piece of timber or iron long in proportion to its thickness, and prepared for use.
A reusable software component written in Java.
A large omnivorous mammal, related to the dog and raccoon, having shaggy hair, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of family Ursidae.
Facial hair on the chin, cheeks, jaw and neck.
Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.
A stroke; a blow.
To hit; strike
example: As soon as she heard that her father had died, she went into a rage and beat the wall with her fists until her knuckles bled.

definitionEn: Someone who is beautiful. Can be used as a term of address.
example: Hey, beautiful!

definitionEn: The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.
example: 1988, "… beauty and recollection, like danger, glamour, greed, hunger- everything but disappointment and desire- were concepts belonging to other people.” -Second Son, Robert Ferro

definitionEn: To arrive, come (to a place).
For the reason (that).
To arrive, come (to a place).
To arrive, come (to a place).
A piece of furniture, usually flat and soft, on which to rest or sleep.
example: My cat often sleeps on my bed. I keep a glass of water next to my bed when I sleep.

definitionEn: A room in a house where a bed is kept for sleeping.
example: Please don't enter my bedroom without knocking.

definitionEn: The time or hour at which one retires to bed in order to sleep.

A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
A tree of the genus Fagus having a smooth, light grey trunk, oval, pointed leaves and many branches.
The meat from a cow, bull or other bovine.
example: I love eating beef.

definitionEn: A 12- to 13-year-old participant in the Young Women organization of the LDS Church.

A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only a minority have them), for collecting pollen and (in some species) producing wax and honey.
An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt, often with hops or some other substance to impart a bitter flavor.
example: Beer is brewed all over the world.

definitionEn: At an earlier time.
example: I've never done this before.

definitionEn: The act of begging; an imploring request.
To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.
example: I began playing the piano at the age of five. Now that everyone is here, we should begin the presentation.

definitionEn: A person who begs.
To request the help of someone, often in the form of money.
example: He begged on the street corner from passers-by.

definitionEn: Beginning; start.

The act of doing that which begins anything; commencement of an action, state, or space of time; entrance into being or upon a course; the first act, effort, or state of a succession of acts or states.
To start, to initiate or take the first step into something.
example: I began playing the piano at the age of five. Now that everyone is here, we should begin the presentation.

definitionEn: To conduct (oneself) well, or in a given way.
example: You need to behave yourself, young lady.

definitionEn: The rear, back-end
A living creature.
To accept as true, particularly without absolute certainty (i.e., as opposed to knowing)
example: I believe there are faeries.

definitionEn: A percussive instrument made of metal or other hard material, typically but not always in the shape of an inverted cup with a flared rim, which resonates when struck.
To have its proper place.
example: Where does this document belong?

definitionEn: In a lower place.
A band worn around the waist to hold clothing to one's body (usually pants), hold weapons (such as a gun or sword), or serve as a decorative piece of clothing.
example: As part of the act, the fat clown's belt broke, causing his pants to fall down.

definitionEn: A long seat with or without a back, found for example in parks and schools.
example: They sat on a park bench and tossed bread crumbs to the ducks and pigeons.

definitionEn: A curve.
example: There's a sharp bend in the road ahead.

definitionEn: Below or underneath.
To cause (something) to change its shape into a curve, by physical force, chemical action, or any other means.
example: Don’t bend your knees.

definitionEn: A small succulent fruit, of any one of many varieties.
Otherwise; else.
Also; in addition.
The supreme effort one can make, or has made.
example: I did my best.

definitionEn: A wager, an agreement between two parties that a stake (usually money) will be paid by the loser to the winner (the winner being the one who correctly forecast the outcome of an event).
example: Dylan owes Fletcher $30 from an unsuccessful bet.

definitionEn: An entity, usually animate, deemed superior to another; one who has a claim to precedence; a superior.
example: He quickly found Ali his better in the ring.

definitionEn: A kind of needle, shorter than a sharp, with a small rounded eye, used for making fine stitches on heavy fabrics.
An item of clothing for people (especially babies) tied around their neck to protect their clothes from getting dirty when eating.
An exemplar of the Bible.
A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
To issue a command; to tell.
example: He bade me come in.

definitionEn: Someone or something that is large in stature
Of great size, large.
example: Elephants are big animals, and they eat a lot.

definitionEn: Any of various bladed or pointed hand weapons, originally designating an Anglo-Saxon sword, and later a weapon of infantry, especially in the 14th and 15th centuries, commonly consisting of a broad, heavy, double-edged, hook-shaped blade, with a short pike at the back and another at the top, attached to the end of a long staff.
A box, frame, crib, or enclosed place, used as a storage container.
example: a corn bin; a wine bin; a coal bin

definitionEn: That which binds or ties.
A member of the class of animals Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs.
example: Ducks and sparrows are birds.

definitionEn: The process of childbearing; the beginning of life.
The anniversary of the day on which someone is born.
(rare in the US) A small, flat, baked good which is either hard and crisp or else soft but firm: a cookie.
A piece of metal placed in a horse's mouth and connected to the reins to direct the animal.
example: A horse hates having a bit put in its mouth.

definitionEn: The act of biting.
(usually in the plural bitters) A liquid or powder, made from bitter herbs, used in mixed drinks or as a tonic.
The colour/color perceived in the absence of light, but also when no light is reflected, but rather absorbed.
A fruit-bearing shrub of the species Rubus fruticosus and some hybrids.
A common true thrush, Turdus merula, found in woods and gardens over much of Eurasia, and introduced elsewhere.
A large flat surface, finished with black slate or a similar material, that can be written upon with chalk and subsequently erased; a chalkboard.
A person who forges iron.
Censure.
example: Blame came from all directions.

definitionEn: A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually large and woollen, used for warmth while sleeping or resting.
example: The baby was cold, so his mother put a blanket over him.

definitionEn: A violent gust of wind.
A fire, especially a fast-burning fire producing a lot of flames and light.
To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.
To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.
To produce an air current.
A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.
A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.
example: a block of ice

definitionEn: A member of the Los Angeles gang The Bloods.
A blossom; the flower of a plant; an expanded bud.
A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
example: The blossom has come early this year.

definitionEn: A blemish, spot or stain made by a coloured substance.
A strong wind.
example: We're having a bit of a blow this afternoon.

definitionEn: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.
An edible round berry, belonging to the cowberry group (Vaccinium sect. Cyanococcus), with flared crowns at the end, that turns blue on ripening.
An act of blushing; a red glow on the face caused by shame, modesty, etc.
A brag; ostentatious positive appraisal of oneself.
A craft used for transportation of goods, fishing, racing, recreational cruising, or military use on or in the water, propelled by oars or outboard motor or inboard motor or by wind.
A bobbing motion; a quick up and down movement.
example: a bob of the head

definitionEn: Physical frame.
A localized accumulation of pus in the skin, resulting from infection.
A dwelling; habitation; building.
A composite material consisting largely of calcium phosphate and collagen and making up the skeleton of most vertebrates.
A type of hat, once worn by women or children, held in place by ribbons tied under the chin.
A derisive shout made to indicate disapproval.
A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
example: He was frustrated because he couldn't find anything about dinosaurs in the book.

definitionEn: A low-pitched, resonant sound, such as of an explosion.
example: The boom of the surf.

definitionEn: A heavy shoe that covers part of the leg.
To carry or convey, literally or figuratively.
example: Judging from the look on his face, he wasn't bearing good news.

definitionEn: Deviation of the path of a rolling ball from a straight line; slope; slant.
example: This putt has a big left-to right borrow on it.

definitionEn: A person who oversees and directs the work of others; a supervisor.
Each of the two, or of the two kinds.
example: "Did you want this one or that one?" — "Give me both."

definitionEn: Fuss, ado.
example: There was a bit of bother at the hairdresser's when they couldn't find my appointment in the book.

definitionEn: A container, typically made of glass or plastic and having a tapered neck, used primarily for holding liquids.
example: Beer is often sold in bottles.

definitionEn: The lowest part of anything.
example: Footers appear at the bottoms of pages.

definitionEn: To obtain (something) in exchange for money or goods
example: I'm going to buy my father something nice for his birthday.

definitionEn: A change of direction of motion after hitting the ground or an obstacle.
A weapon made of a curved piece of wood or other flexible material whose ends are connected by a string, used for shooting arrows.
A roughly hemispherical container used to hold, mix or present food, such as salad, fruit or soup, or other items.
Senses relating to a three-dimensional object or space.
Senses relating to a three-dimensional object or space.
A young male.
example: Kate is dating a boy named Jim.

definitionEn: The control center of the central nervous system of an animal located in the skull which is responsible for perception, cognition, attention, memory, emotion, and action.
A fern; bracken.
The broken coat of the seed of wheat, rye, or other cereal grain, separated from the flour or meal by sifting or bolting; the coarse, chaffy part of ground grain.
The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing.
A metallic alloy of copper and zinc used in many industrial and plumbing applications.
A Native American warrior.
A foodstuff made by baking dough made from cereals.
An instance of breaking something into two or more pieces.
example: The femur has a clean break and so should heal easily.

definitionEn: The first meal of the day, usually eaten in the morning.
example: You should put more protein in her breakfast so she will grow.

definitionEn: Either of the two organs on the front of a female human's chest, which contain the mammary glands; also the analogous organs in males.
example: Tanya's breasts grew remarkably during pregnancy.

definitionEn: The act or process of breathing.
example: I could hear the breath of the runner behind me.

definitionEn: To draw air into (inhale), and expel air from (exhale), the lungs in order to extract oxygen and excrete waste gases.
A light, gentle wind.
example: The breeze rustled the papers on her desk.

definitionEn: A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
example: This wall is made of bricks.

definitionEn: A construction or natural feature that spans a divide.
An artist's brush used in oil and acrylic painting with a long ferrule and a flat, somewhat tapering bristle head.
The quality of being bright.
(ditransitive) To transport toward somebody/somewhere.
example: Waiter, please bring me a single malt whiskey.

definitionEn: A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.
A transmission of a radio or television programme intended to be received by anyone with a receiver.
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitionEn: To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitionEn: To use; enjoy; have the full employment of.
A domestic utensil with fibers bound together at the end of a long handle, used for sweeping.
Son of the same parents as another person.
(ditransitive) To transport toward somebody/somewhere.
example: Waiter, please bring me a single malt whiskey.

definitionEn: A colour like that of chocolate or coffee.
example: The browns and greens in this painting give it a nice woodsy feel.

definitionEn: An implement consisting of multiple more or less flexible bristles or other filaments attached to a handle, used for any of various purposes including cleaning, painting, and arranging hair.
A spherically contained volume of air or other gas, especially one made from soapy liquid.
A container made of rigid material, often with a handle, used to carry liquids or small items.
example: I need a bucket to carry the water from the well.

definitionEn: To distort or collapse under physical pressure; especially, of a slender structure in compression.
A newly sprouted leaf or blossom that has not yet unfolded.
example: After a long, cold winter, the trees finally began to produce buds.
